fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alabama Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alabama - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity alabama, how to transfer pharmacist license to alabama, pharmacist compact alabama, alabama pharmacist endorsement application

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Alabama.

steps.title: Create NABP e-Profile and Start Application
steps.description: Visit nabp.pharmacy, create an e-Profile ID and password, and then log in to start the Licensure Transfer Program (LTP) application, ensuring you select Alabama as the receiving state.

steps.title: Complete NABP Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out the NABP Licensure Transfer Application completely, provide all required documentation, and pay the associated NABP application processing fee.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Request a fingerprint packet from the Alabama Board of Pharmacy (ALBOP) office and follow the instructions to complete a criminal background check via fingerprinting.

steps.title: Pass the MPJE
steps.description: Register for and successfully pass the Multistate Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) for Alabama with a score of 75% or greater.

steps.title: Submit Additional ALBOP Documents and Fee
steps.description: Once your NABP application is forwarded, pay the Alabama Board of Pharmacy's licensure fee of $100 and submit any additional state-specific forms or information requested directly by the ALBOP to complete your application.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Alabama pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Alabama have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Alabama. To work in Alabama, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Alabama?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alabama while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Alabama
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in Alabama by endorsement, users must apply through the NABP Licensure Transfer Program (LTP) portal, in addition to fulfilling Alabama-specific requirements.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Create an NABP e-Profile ID and password on nabp.pharmacy if you don't already have one. Log in to your e-Profile, select the 'Licensure Transfer' button on the Programs page, and follow the prompts to start your application.
